description: Extend /instinct-evolve and the background Haiku analyzer with skill promotion suggestions, AGENTS.md duplication prevention (two-layer), AGENTS.md addition suggestions, installed-skill deduplication, and deep action-text instinct deduplication.

userStories.title: Deep Instinct Deduplication
userStories.description: Rename tokenizeTrigger to tokenizeText, add actionSimilarity(), extend findMergeCandidates with an action-text similarity pass, add recommendation/keepId fields to MergeSuggestion, and split instinct-evolve.ts into handler+formatter (<200 lines) and instinct-evolve-generators.ts (<400 lines).
acceptanceCriteria: tokenizeTrigger is renamed to tokenizeText and re-exported from instinct-evolve-generators.ts
All callers of tokenizeTrigger (triggerSimilarity, tests) are updated to use tokenizeText
actionSimilarity(a, b) computes Jaccard similarity on action text token sets using tokenizeText
findMergeCandidates runs a second pass comparing action text with threshold 0.4; pairs already caught by trigger pass (threshold 0.3) are not duplicated
MergeSuggestion includes recommendation: 'merge' | 'delete-lower' and keepId: string (higher-confidence instinct's id; tie-breaks to first alphabetically)
instinct-evolve.ts contains only the command handler (handleInstinctEvolve) and formatter (formatEvolveSuggestions) - under 200 lines
instinct-evolve-generators.ts contains all findX() and generateEvolveSuggestions() functions - under 400 lines
All existing tests for tokenizeTrigger are updated to tokenizeText; new tests cover actionSimilarity and the action-based dedup pass
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: This story also performs the module split described in the PRD technical considerations section. Do the split first, then add the new logic.

userStories.title: Analyzer AGENTS.md Awareness (Prevention Layer)
userStories.description: Create a readAgentsMd() utility, extend buildAnalyzerUserPrompt with agentsMdProject/agentsMdGlobal/installedSkills parameters, and add 'Avoid Duplicating Guidelines' and 'Avoid Duplicating Installed Skills' sections to the analyzer system prompt so Haiku skips creating instincts already covered by AGENTS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: agents-md.ts exports readAgentsMd(filePath: string): string | null - returns file content or null if not found/unreadable
buildAnalyzerUserPrompt accepts agentsMdProject: string | null, agentsMdGlobal: string | null, installedSkills: Array<{name: string; description: string}> as optional parameters
When agentsMdProject or agentsMdGlobal are non-null, their content is appended under '## Existing Guidelines' in the user prompt
When installedSkills is non-empty, the list (name + description) is appended under '## Installed Skills' in the user prompt
buildAnalyzerSystemPrompt includes a new 'Avoid Duplicating Guidelines' section instructing Haiku to skip patterns already covered by AGENTS.md
buildAnalyzerSystemPrompt includes a new 'Avoid Duplicating Installed Skills' section instructing Haiku not to create instincts for behaviors covered by a named skill
InstalledSkill interface is added to types.ts: { name: string; description: string }
Unit tests cover: readAgentsMd with existing file, missing file, unreadable file; new user prompt parameters appear in output; new system prompt sections are present
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: InstalledSkill type added here (FR-10) since it is also needed by US-005 and US-001. No changes to index.ts or instinct-evolve in this story.

userStories.title: /instinct-evolve AGENTS.md Overlap Flagging (Post-hoc Layer)
userStories.description: Extend /instinct-evolve to read project and global AGENTS.md at command run time and flag any existing instincts whose trigger+action text overlaps lexically with AGENTS.md content.
acceptanceCriteria: handleInstinctEvolve accepts projectRoot: string | null as a new parameter; index.ts supplies it from session context
At invocation, project AGENTS.md ({projectRoot}/AGENTS.md) and global AGENTS.md (~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md) are read via readAgentsMd()
AGENTS_MD_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD = 0.6 constant is defined in instinct-evolve-generators.ts (or config.ts)
findAgentsMdOverlaps(instincts, agentsMdText): AgentsMdOverlapSuggestion[] checks each instinct - if >= 60% of significant tokens from trigger+action appear in agentsMdText, the instinct is flagged
AgentsMdOverlapSuggestion has type: 'agents-md-overlap', instinct: Instinct, matchingExcerpt: string (up to 100 chars of the matching AGENTS.md portion)
Flagged instincts appear in a 'Duplicates AGENTS.md' section in the evolve output, showing id, confidence, and excerpt
If no AGENTS.md files exist or both are null, the section is omitted silently
generateEvolveSuggestions is updated to accept and thread agentsMd parameters
Unit tests use fixture AGENTS.md strings to verify overlap detection at/above/below threshold
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: Uses readAgentsMd() from US-002. AGENTS_MD_OVERLAP_THRESHOLD may go in config.ts or instinct-evolve-generators.ts - either is acceptable.

userStories.title: AGENTS.md Addition Suggestions
userStories.description: Extend /instinct-evolve to suggest which high-confidence instincts are ready to be graduated into permanent AGENTS.md project or global guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: findAgentsMdAdditions(instincts, overlapIds, scope): AgentsMdAdditionSuggestion[] returns instincts that pass confidence threshold and are NOT already flagged as overlapping AGENTS.md
AgentsMdAdditionSuggestion has type: 'agents-md-addition', instinct: Instinct, proposedBullet: string (derived from trigger + action, plain English), scope: 'project' | 'global'
Project-scoped instincts with confidence >= 0.75 not in overlapIds are suggested as project AGENTS.md additions
Global-scoped instincts with confidence >= 0.8 not in overlapIds are suggested as global AGENTS.md additions
Output includes two sub-sections: 'Suggested Project AGENTS.md Additions' and 'Suggested Global AGENTS.md Additions'; each is omitted when empty
Each suggestion shows: instinct id, confidence, and the proposed bullet text with a note that the user must edit the file manually
No AGENTS.md file is written or modified
Unit tests cover: confidence threshold boundary, overlap exclusion, both scopes, empty results
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: overlapIds is the set of instinct IDs already flagged by US-003 overlap detection, passed from generateEvolveSuggestions.

userStories.title: Installed Skills Deduplication
userStories.description: Discover installed Pi skills at session start, pass them to both the analyzer and /instinct-evolve, and flag instincts that are already covered by an installed skill.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL_DOMAINS constant is added to config.ts mapping domain names to human-readable purposes (e.g., git, testing, debugging, workflow, typescript)

index.ts attempts to discover installed skills at session_start: try pi.getInstalledSkills() (or equivalent Pi SDK API) inside a try-catch; fall back to scanning ~/.pi/agent/skills/*/SKILL.md and known pi-package skill directories, parsing name+description from YAML frontmatter; fall back to empty array if all fails
A TODO comment in index.ts notes the actual Pi SDK API needs verification
The discovered skill list is stored in extension state and passed to buildAnalyzerUserPrompt as installedSkills
The discovered skill list is passed to handleInstinctEvolve

findSkillShadows(instincts, installedSkills): SkillShadowSuggestion[] checks each instinct - if instinct domain matches a SKILL_DOMAINS key that matches a skill name, or trigger tokens overlap >= 0.3 with skill name+description tokens, the instinct is flagged
SkillShadowSuggestion has type: 'skill-shadow', instinct: Instinct, skillName: string
Flagged instincts appear in a 'Shadowed by Installed Skill' section in evolve output
If installedSkills is empty, the section is omitted silently
Unit tests for findSkillShadows use hardcoded skill lists (no Pi API); cover domain match, token overlap match, no-match cases
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: InstalledSkill interface was added in US-002. SKILL_DOMAINS goes in config.ts. The Pi SDK skill API is unknown - wrap in try-catch and document with TODO.

userStories.title: Skill Promotion Suggestions
userStories.description: Extend /instinct-evolve to identify instincts (individually or as merge-candidate clusters) that have enough coherence and confidence to be formalized into a Pi skill file.
acceptanceCriteria: findSkillPromotionCandidates(instincts, mergeSuggestions, shadowIds): SkillPromotionSuggestion[] identifies candidates
A single instinct qualifies if: confidence >= 0.8 AND domain maps to a key in SKILL_DOMAINS AND its id is NOT in shadowIds (not already covered by an installed skill)
A merge-candidate cluster qualifies if: all member instincts have confidence >= 0.7 AND they share a common domain in SKILL_DOMAINS AND no member id is in shadowIds
SkillPromotionSuggestion has type: 'skill-promotion', instincts: Instinct[], reason: string (includes suggested skill trigger/purpose), domain: string
Output includes a 'Skill Promotion Candidates' section listing qualifying instincts/clusters with their IDs, confidence scores, and a note that no skill file is written
If no candidates exist or all are suppressed by installed skills, the section is omitted
generateEvolveSuggestions is updated to thread shadowIds from SkillShadowSuggestions into findSkillPromotionCandidates
Unit tests cover: single-instinct candidate, cluster candidate, suppression by installed skill, domain not in SKILL_DOMAINS
npx vitest run && npx eslint src/ && npx tsc --noEmit all pass
userStories.notes: shadowIds is the set of instinct IDs already flagged as skill-shadowed by US-005, passed from generateEvolveSuggestions to suppress overlapping promotion suggestions.
